title: Celtics legend Paul Pierce continues to make a case for Jaylen Brown as NBA MVP
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Celtics legend Paul Pierce advocates for Jaylen Brown as a strong candidate for NBA MVP honors.
Jaylen Brown is averaging 28.5 points, 7.0 rebounds, and 5.2 assists per game this season.
Boston Celtics are in second place in the Eastern Conference with a 47-23 record, aided by Brown's performance.

title: Plymouth police officer on paid leave amid criminal investigation
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Plymouth police officer Samantha Pelrine is on paid leave amid a State Police criminal investigation into off-duty conduct.
The investigation into Pelrine's conduct was initiated by Massachusetts State Police, with details currently undisclosed.
Plymouth Police Department is conducting an internal investigation, emphasizing accountability and policy compliance.

title: MLB writer predicts Roman Anthony will win AL MVP this season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Roman Anthony predicted to win AL MVP in 2026 by The Athletic's Eno Sarris.
Anthony impressed in 2025 with .292 average, 8 home runs, 32 RBIs in 71 games.
Anthony excelled in the 2026 World Baseball Classic, batting .280 with 7 RBIs for Team USA.

title: Pedestrian killed in Roxbury hit-and-run
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Pedestrian killed in a hit-and-run near Southampton and Bradston streets in Roxbury, Boston on Friday morning.
Police found the vehicle involved in the incident, but the driver fled and remains unidentified.
No arrests have been made yet, and the investigation into the hit-and-run is ongoing.

title: Here’s what meteorologists are saying about chances for snow through Monday
author: Jack Pickell
bullets: Meteorologists predict 1–2 inches of wet snow around I-90, with a mix of rain and snow Monday morning.
Rain transitions to wet snow early Monday across northern MA, CT, RI, and parts of southeastern MA.
Brief snow showers expected Monday night, with potential coatings on grassy surfaces, especially in higher elevations.

title: Mass. police officer arrested for OUI, department says
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Norwood Police Officer Shawn Willman was arrested and charged with operating under the influence (OUI) on Thursday.
Willman was off duty at the time of the arrest and has been placed on administrative leave, pending case outcome.
Norwood Police are conducting an internal investigation; no court information available yet as of Sunday evening.

title: Police: Mass. man allegedly stabbed friend during fight over woman at Miami Beach bachelor party
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Olajuwon Shawn Dickerson, 32, of Marston Mills, charged with aggravated battery at Miami Beach bachelor party.
Dickerson allegedly stabbed friend Brian Gordon during argument over women, both were injured and hospitalized.
Police recovered a blood-covered folding knife allegedly used in the incident; Dickerson denies owning the knife.

title: 4 takeaways from the Revolution’s 3-1 loss to St. Louis City SC
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The New England Revolution lost 3-1 to St. Louis City SC, despite an early lead from Ilay Feingold.
Revolution's defense struggled with set pieces, conceding a crucial goal from a corner by Chris Durkin.
Forward Leo Campana sustained a new injury after being subbed on, unrelated to his previous two-week absence.

title: Don’t be fooled by this fake court notice scam in Mass., NH, authorities warn
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Authorities in Massachusetts and New Hampshire warn about fake court notices sent by scammers.
The notices contain errors, such as misspelled state names, and direct victims to pay fines via a QR code.
Recipients are advised to ignore these notices and verify any concerns through official court websites or phone numbers.

title: The trial of Kelsey Fitzsimmons begins this week. Here’s what to know.
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Kelsey Fitzsimmons, a North Andover police officer, was shot in her home by a colleague during an armed confrontation.
Fitzsimmons has pleaded not guilty to assault with a dangerous weapon; her trial begins March 23 with a bench trial by Judge Jeffrey Karp.
Conflicting reports and allegations of misconduct and mental health issues have complicated the case involving a restraining order and custody battle.
